quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 4 Quiz 11
quiz.subtitle: Mixed Review of Triangle Concepts
quiz.subject: Geometry
quiz.topic: Geometry: 4.11 Problem-Solving with Triangles and Congruence

questions.question: In $\triangle XYZ$, the measure of $\angle X$ is $43^\circ$ and the measure of $\angle Y$ is $74^\circ$. What is the measure of the exterior angle at vertex $Z$?
questions.explanation: By the Exterior Angle Theorem, the measure of an exterior angle of a triangle equals the sum of the measures of its two remote interior angles. Here, $43^\circ + 74^\circ = 117^\circ$.

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has a vertex angle measuring $52^\circ$. What is the measure of one of its base angles?
questions.explanation: The interior angles of a triangle sum to $180^\circ$. Subtracting the vertex angle gives $180^\circ - 52^\circ = 128^\circ$. Since the base angles of an isosceles triangle are congruent, each measures $64^\circ$.

questions.question: In $\triangle ABC$, $\overline{AB} = 2x + 9$ and $\overline{BC} = 5x - 18$. If $\triangle ABC$ is equilateral, what is the length of $\overline{AC}$?
questions.explanation: Since the triangle is equilateral, all sides are equal. Setting $2x + 9 = 5x - 18$ gives $3x = 27$, so $x = 9$. Substituting: $2(9) + 9 = 27$.

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle measures $146^\circ$. If one of its remote interior angles measures $82^\circ$, what is the measure of the other remote interior angle?
questions.explanation: By the Exterior Angle Theorem, the exterior angle equals the sum of the two remote interior angles: $146^\circ = 82^\circ + x$. Solving gives $x = 64^\circ$.

questions.question: Two sides of an isosceles triangle have lengths $7$ cm and $15$ cm. What is the perimeter of the triangle?
questions.explanation: By the Triangle Inequality Theorem, sides of $7, 7, 15$ are impossible because $7 + 7 < 15$. The sides must be $15, 15, 7$. The perimeter is $15 + 15 + 7 = 37$ cm.

questions.question: If $\triangle ABC \cong \triangle DEF$, $m\angle A = 57^\circ$, and $m\angle E = 68^\circ$, what is $m\angle C$?
questions.explanation: By CPCTC, $\angle B = \angle E = 68^\circ$. Using the Triangle Angle Sum Theorem: $m\angle C = 180^\circ - (57^\circ + 68^\circ) = 55^\circ$.

questions.question: In right triangle $\triangle DEF$, the hypotenuse $\overline{DF} = 13$ cm and leg $\overline{DE} = 5$ cm. If $\triangle DEF \cong \triangle LMN$, what is the length of $\overline{LN}$?
questions.explanation: By the Pythagorean Theorem, the missing leg $EF = \sqrt{13^2 - 5^2} = \sqrt{169 - 25} = \sqrt{144} = 12$ cm. By CPCTC, the corresponding leg $\overline{LN}$ is also $12$ cm.

questions.question: In overlapping triangles $\triangle ACD$ and $\triangle BCE$, it is given that $\triangle ACD \cong \triangle BCE$. If $\overline{AD} = 3x + 11$ and $\overline{BE} = 7x - 13$, what is the value of $x$?
An equiangular triangle has a side length of $4x - 3$. If the perimeter is $45$ units, what is the value of $x$?
questions.explanation: An equiangular triangle is equilateral, so all three sides are equal. Each side is $45 \div 3 = 15$. Setting $4x - 3 = 15$ gives $4x = 18$, so $x = 4.5$.

questions.question: In $\triangle KLM$, a segment from vertex $K$ bisects $\overline{LM}$ at point $N$, creating $\triangle KNL \cong \triangle KNM$. If $\overline{LN} = 3x - 2$ and $\overline{LM} = 32$, what is the value of $x$?

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have a strong command of triangle properties, congruence, and the theorems that connect them.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the core concepts but review the Exterior Angle Theorem and the Triangle Inequality Theorem in the context of isosceles triangles.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: Triangle Angle Sum Theorem, Exterior Angle Theorem, Base Angles Theorem, CPCTC, and the Pythagorean Theorem for right triangles.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Review triangle classifications, the Triangle Angle Sum Theorem ($180^\circ$), the Exterior Angle Theorem, congruence postulates (SSS, SAS, ASA, AAS, HL), and CPCTC.
